channel.title: Azure Functions on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Azure Functions on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Python 3.14
item.description:
Azure Functions now supports Python 3.14 for local development and deployment to Linux-based Azure Functions plans. This lets apps move to Python 3.14 for security, longer support, and continued compatibility.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Functions Support for PowerShell 7.6
item.description:
Azure Functions now supports PowerShell 7.6 in public preview. You can develop locally with PowerShell 7.6 and deploy to Azure Functions plans.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: New project templates and template gallery for Azure Functions in VS Code
item.description:
The Azure Functions VS Code extension adds a redesigned Create New Project experience in public preview. It replaces the multi-step Quick Pick wizard with a searchable, filterable visual template gallery.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Rolling updates in Flex Consumption
item.description:
Rolling updates are now generally available in Azure Functions Flex Consumption. They allow code and configuration changes to be applied without force-restarting all instances, enabling zero-downtime deployments with a simple configuration change.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Functions now includes managed connectors to integrate with 1400+ of your favorite systems
item.description:
Azure Functions Public Preview now supports managed connectors as first-class triggers and operations. This brings access to the same 1,400+ connector ecosystem used by Logic Apps and Power Platform for integrating with external systems.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Copilot-assisted coding & agent skills to build, deploy, and run Azure Functions faster in VS Code, CLI, and azd
item.description:
Azure Functions public preview adds Copilot-assisted coding and agent skills for building, deploying, and running Functions apps in VS Code, the CLI, and azd. The preview aims to improve AI help quality by providing Azure Functions-specific guidance.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Go language support on Azure Functions
item.description:
Azure Functions now supports Go as a first-class language option in public preview. Developers can build event-driven serverless apps in Go using the Azure Functions Go programming model and SDK.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Serverless agents runtime for Azure Functions
item.description:
Azure Functions adds a public preview for serverless agents runtime, positioning AI agents as a first-class workload. The model is markdown-first and event-driven, allowing agents to be authored in natural language.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions now supports hosting MCP Apps
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports hosting MCP apps. The update notes that MCP servers can use richer interactions beyond plain text, including visual data exploration, complex configuration, real-time status monitoring, and multi-step workflows.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Built-in Grafana dashboards for Azure Functions
item.description:
Azure Functions now includes built-in Grafana dashboards in generally available form. The dashboards provide Azure portal visibility into function app health, performance, and scale without needing to provision Grafana or configure data sources.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Override Scale Rules in Azure Functions on Azure Container Apps
item.description:
Azure Functions on Azure Container Apps now supports overriding platform-managed scaling rules through the new allowScalingRuleOverride property. This lets customers replace the automatically translated KEDA scale rules with their own scaling configuration.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions Support for Node.js 24
item.description:
Azure Functions now supports Node.js 24 in general availability. Apps can be developed locally with Node.js 24 and deployed to supported Azure Functions plans on Linux and Windows, including Flex Consumption.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 in Azure App Service, Azure Functions, and Azure Logic Apps
item.description:
Azure App Service, Azure Functions, and Azure Logic Apps will stop accepting TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 connections after May 31, 2027. Clients and services must use TLS 1.2 or later to continue connecting.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: TLS/SSL certificate support for Azure Functions Flex Consumption
item.description:
Azure Functions Flex Consumption now supports TLS/SSL certificates in public preview using a site-scoped certificate model. This differs from webspace-scoped certificates on other plans, which are shared across apps in the same region and resource group.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Durable Workflows in the Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now supports durable AI agent workflows using the Durable Task runtime. The post shows a progression from in-process console apps to parallel agent execution and Azure Functions hosting for serverless runs.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions durable task scheduler Consumption SKU
item.description:
The Durable Task Scheduler Consumption SKU for Azure Functions is now generally available. It supports durable workflows and AI agent orchestrations with pay-per-use pricing and no storage, capacity planning, or idle costs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Java 25
item.description:
Azure Functions now generally supports Java 25. You can develop and deploy Java 25 apps locally to supported Azure Functions plans on Linux and Windows, including Flex Consumption.

  • Source: Azure Updates
